findings.detail: Node count 6 (within 3–8). All bold labels ≤ 4 words: 'Incident', 'What Failed', 'Why It Failed', 'Who Was Accountable', 'Governance Fix', 'No-Blame Review'. Title 'Post-Mortem Writing Process' sits in the YAML config title: field as the governing concept — not a ghost node. No bare unexplained acronyms present.

A 'Incident' → §6 step 1 'Choose incident'; B 'What Failed' → §5 Section 1 'factual description of system, failure event, scope, and harm' + §6 step 2; C 'Why It Failed' → §5 Section 2 'proximate cause + root cause' with all four root cause categories (training data problem, evaluation gap, missing checkpoint, accountability gap) referenced in subtitle + §6 steps 3a-3b; D 'Who Was Accountable' → §5 Section 3 'was accountability real or diffuse?' + §6 step 4; E 'Governance Fix' → §5 Section 4 'specific, placed, proportionate governance control' + §6 step 5; F 'No-Blame Review' → §6 step 6 'Review for no-blame framing'. No scope-creep nodes — HITL, risk tiers, and other 10.5/10.6 concepts are absent.

One straight A→B→C→D→E→F chain. The corpus describes a 6-step linear writing procedure (§6), so a single-path pipeline is the correct layout — no branches needed. The 'Why It Failed' node's subtitle surfaces the four root cause categories inline rather than as branch nodes, avoiding layout complexity while preserving the information.

A:::start (blue #a5d8ff) — entry/trigger event. B/C/D/E:::auto (purple #d0bfff) — the four analytical writing sections the post-mortem author works through. F:::done (green #b2f2bb) — terminal quality-check step. Color tier mapping is semantically clean: the trigger is blue (start), the four procedure steps are purple (process/auto), and the final review gate is green (done). No hitl nodes used because no human-gate decision point is encoded in the diagram — the post-mortem sections are writing steps, not approval gates.

6-node linear LR chain matches the intent: 'Four-phase post-mortem flow: Incident → Root Cause Analysis → Accountability Mapping → Governance Fix'. The 'Incident' node serves as the trigger before the first section; 'No-Blame Review' is the final step from §6. At 1400px width with 6 nodes, estimated layout will comfortably fit without truncation risk.

Plain-language labels match how the corpus terms the four sections (no technical jargon). Subtitles use plain phrases ('The event that happened', 'Factual description of system, scope & harm'). The four root cause categories are named in subtitle as short tokens, matching the way the corpus names them. Beginner-readable.

mmdc not available on PATH in this environment (consistent with established repo pattern — see 10-1, 10-2, 10-3, 10-4, 10-5 critic reports). Source pre-flight passes all checks. PNG render to be executed by orchestrator or CI step using: mmdc -i <mmd-path> -o <png-path> -w 1400 -b transparent. nodeSpacing:60 / rankSpacing:80 in the config block will provide adequate separation for 6 nodes with multi-line htmlLabels at 1400px width.

preflight.mermaid_parse: source review pass (mmdc not available for exit-code check)
preflight.concept_fidelity: pass (all labels match corpus §5 Core Concepts + §6 Implementation; on-headline, no scope-creep)
preflight.title_discipline: pass (governing concept in title: field, no ghost node)
preflight.single_path: pass (A→B→C→D→E→F, no branches — linear 6-step writing procedure)
